title: 10 best herbal teas for dry cough

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glycyrrhetic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, soothing a dry and irritated cough.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in licorice root have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Soother of mucous membranes: Licorice tea's demulcent properties help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing irritation and inflammation, and providing relief from a dry and scratchy cough.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing properties and ability to reduce inflammation in the throat. The roots of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from dry, persistent coughs. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the respiratory system, making it an effective natural remedy for dry coughs.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with dry cough by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and suppressing cough reflex due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Liquiritin: This phenolic compound helps with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) activities, which can help soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Licoricidin: This flavonoid glycoside helps with dry cough by showing antitussive and expectorant activities, which can help relieve cough and promote mucus secretion to help clear the airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe a dry cough.
Leaves: Used for their bitter and sweet compounds, which aid in reducing cough and promoting expectoration.
Roots: Also used because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate dry cough symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 bunches of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Clean the roots by rinsing them with filtered water.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh filter or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help combat the underlying bacterial infections that cause dry coughs, reducing their severity and duration.

Expectorant Properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm, providing relief from dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms, and alleviate discomfort and pain associated with coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with dry cough because it contains thymol and carvacrol, compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and combat the underlying infection causing the cough. As a natural expectorant, thyme tea helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from dry, persistent coughs. Its calming effects on the respiratory system also promote a restful night's sleep, further aiding in the recovery from dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: A phenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving dry cough symptoms.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and reduce dry cough.
Bornyl acetate: A monoterpene that has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to thin mucus and relieve dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, particularly thymol, which has decongestant and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and volatile oils, including thymol, that help ease coughs and congestion due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: High in essential oils, including thymol, which helps to reduce inflammation and clear mucus from the airways, relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a pot until it reaches a rolling boil in 3 minutes.
Steep the thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves.
Serve the tea hot or iced and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and alleviate dry cough symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in peppermint tea help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate dry coughs, while also soothing irritated mucous membranes.
Expectorant properties: Peppermint tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs and bronchi, providing relief from stubborn dry coughs and congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The menthol content in peppermint helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe an irritated throat, providing relief from a dry, hacking cough. As a natural and non-irritating remedy, peppermint teas can be an effective and gentle solution for dry cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a terpene that helps relieve dry cough by creating a cooling sensation in the throat, providing temporary relief from irritation and inflammation.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a phenolic compound that helps with dry cough by acting as an expectorant, loosening mucus and reducing congestion, thereby making it easier to cough up.
Menthone: Menthone is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the throat and suppress coughing, which can provide relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are the most commonly used part to make teas for dry cough due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a soothing effect on the respiratory system.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Mentha x piperita are used to make teas for dry cough as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to leaves, offering a more intense and long-lasting relief.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make teas for dry cough, although to a lesser extent, as they also contain menthol and menthone, although in smaller quantities.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of mentha x piperita in quantities of about 1 to 2 teaspoons.
Heat water in a kettle to a temperature of around 212 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Steep the leaves in the heated water for 5 to 10 minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves and other solids from the tea.
Serve the tea hot or cold as desired and enjoy its refreshing flavor and aroma immediately.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: The flavonoids and triterpenes present in Kansas Coneflower tea have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause dry cough.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The saponins and phenolic acids in the tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, alleviating symptoms of dry cough.

Mucilage production: Kansas Coneflower contains a type of soluble fiber called mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dry cough.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with dry cough because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The root of the plant is traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tracts, reducing inflammation and relieving congestion. The active ingredients in Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and glycosides, may also help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of coughs caused by viral infections, providing quick relief from dry, persistent coughs.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to dry cough, and also exhibit anti-tussive properties, directly relieving cough symptoms.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and soothe the throat, which can help alleviate dry cough.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its clearance from the airways, which can help alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make teas for dry cough due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make teas for dry cough due to their flavonoid content, which may help to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat.

Roots and Stems are often used together, but if you want to pick a third, Stems: The stems of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make teas for dry cough due to their alkaloid content, which may help to reduce mucus production and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 to 3 teaspoons of dried echinacea angustifolia root in a clean bowl.
Combine the root with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate cup.
Drink the tea immediately and consume 3 to 4 times a day.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lemon verbena tea helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of a dry cough by decreasing swelling and irritation.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antiviral and antimicrobial properties help to combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause dry coughs, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Herbal lemon verbena tea's expectorant and mucolytic properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing and expectorant properties. The tea's high content of volatile oils, particularly citral and geranial, helps to break down mucus and ease congestion, providing relief from dry coughs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Verbena officinalis tea help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and promoting a more comfortable cough. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from dry coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Verbenalin: This iridoid glycoside has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve dry cough by facilitating the removal of mucus from the airways and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Verbascoside: This phenylethanoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, helping to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieving dry cough.

Isorhapontigenin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, protect against oxidative stress, and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Flowers: They are used for their sweet fragrance and medicinal properties, which help to reduce cough and provide relief from dryness in the throat.

Roots: They are used as they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, such as iridoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to alleviate dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their most fragrant and potent.
Rinse the verbena leaves and flowers with cool water to remove dirt and any impurities instantly.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried verbena leaves and flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh verbena leaves and flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the verbena leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea and discard the verbena leaves and flowers before serving the tea hot or iced.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Amla tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving discomfort associated with dry cough.
Expectorant properties: The tea's natural expectorants (such as vitamin C and flavonoids) help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Amla tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus emblica, also known as amla, teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The high concentration of vitamin C in amla helps to reduce inflammation in the throat, providing quick relief from dry cough. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of amla help to soothe and calm the irritated throat, allowing for easy mucus secretion and cough relief. Regular consumption of amla tea has been shown to provide long-term relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Ellagic acid: Ellagic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity of dry cough.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as gallic acid, have antimicrobial properties that help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the dry cough, thereby reducing its severity.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are commonly used to make teas for dry cough due to their high concentration of ascorbic acid, which helps to soothe and calm the throat.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make teas for dry cough because of their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and calm the respiratory system.
Barks: Barks are used to make teas for dry cough due to their antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections and reduce the severity of coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried phyllanthus emblica fruits from the trees in the summer season.
Rinse 10 grams of the dried phyllanthus emblica fruits with cold water for a minute.
Steep the rinsed phyllanthus emblica fruits in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the steeped mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Serve the phyllanthus emblica tea hot or chilled within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing relief from dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in dandelion tea acts as a natural expectorant, loosening mucus and phlegm in the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion caused by dry cough.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry cough and other respiratory issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a prebiotic that promotes digestive health, and flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, providing quick relief from dry cough. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and breathe more comfortably. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin helps with dry cough by soothing and lubricating the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and inflammation.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving dry cough symptoms.

Taraxasterol acetate: Another triterpenoid saponin, taraxasterol acetate exhibits anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties that help relax the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing spasms and coughing fits associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe a dry cough.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties to help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
Flowers: Employed for their expectorant and antiseptic properties to help relieve congestion and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a clean source in a ratio of 2:1 leaves to flowers.
Rinse the gathered taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers under cold running water for 30 seconds to remove impurities.
Heat 250ml of boiling water and let it steep with 2-3 teaspoons of taraxacum officinale for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove solids.
Store the herbal taraxacum officinale tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours in an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Rosemary tea helps to soothe and calm a dry cough, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort, making it easier to breathe and sleep.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in rosemary tea help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and providing relief from stubborn coughs.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating dryness and irritation that can contribute to a persistent cough.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, teas helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, rosemary tea has antispasmodic properties that calm the throat muscles, reducing the severity of coughing fits. remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as Eucalyptol): It helps relieve dry cough by acting as an expectorant, thinning mucus and making it easier to cough up.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate dry cough.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Rosmarinus officinalis for teas to alleviate dry cough, as they contain essential oils and antioxidants that help soothe the throat.

Buds: The buds of Rosmarinus officinalis are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain a high concentration of essential oils that help to relieve cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from the plant.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool water to remove any impurities and debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature immediately.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in mallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.

Demulcent activity: Mallow tea's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, trapping moisture and reducing inflammation, which helps to soothe and calm dry coughs, allowing for more effective healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Malva sylvestris, also known as mallow, teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing and demulcent properties. The mucilage in the plant forms a protective barrier on the throat and respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to calm the dry, scratchy sensation associated with dry coughs, while also providing relief from congestion and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Malva sylvestris also help to reduce coughing, allowing for more comfortable breathing and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with dry cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and expectorating mucus, making it easier to cough up.

Phenolic acids (specifically, gallic acid): Gallic acid helps with dry cough by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing the severity of coughing and soothing irritated mucous membranes.

Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid): Ursolic acid helps with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, breaking down and dissolving mucus, and making it easier to expel.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain anthraquinones and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and calm dry coughs.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, similar to the leaves, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried malva sylvestris flowers or 20-30 grams of fresh leaves and flowers.
Steep the gathered herb in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the herb particles.
Discard the solids and transfer the remaining liquid to a clean container for serving.
Store the prepared tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Elder tea contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help combat the underlying infection causing the dry cough by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and reducing the severity of dry cough symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with dry cough.

This can provide quick relief from dry, hacking coughs by loosening and clearing out mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in coughing fits. Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic and ferulic acids): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections leading to dry cough.

Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid): This compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up excess mucus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in saponins, which help in loosening and removing mucus from the airways, providing relief from dry cough.
Buds: The buds of Sambucus nigra contain glycosides, which are believed to help in reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh sambucus nigra branches in late spring or early summer.
Dry the harvested branches in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or use a food.
Crush 2 tablespoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the sambucus nigra powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7.
Strain the tea and enjoy immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated throat. The warm liquid helps to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help to thin and expel phlegm, making it easier to breathe and relieving the persistent coughing spasms. This natural remedy provides instant relief, calming the throat and chest while promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and bronchial passages, which can alleviate the discomfort and dryness associated with a dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The volatile oils present in ginger, such as citral and limonene, have been shown to increase mucus production and help loosen congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm from the lungs and relieving a dry, hacking cough.

Antitussive properties: Ginger's active compounds, including gingerol and paradol, possess antitussive properties that can help soothe an irritated throat and reduce the urge to cough, providing relief from a persistent dry cough.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help relieve congestion and soothe a dry cough.
Roots: Containing natural expectorants, roots help loosen mucus and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale of good quality.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry sliced or chopped ginger roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve flavor.
Steep one teaspoon of dried ginger powder or two slices of fresh ginger in one cup boiling distilled water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey if desired then drink immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The camphor and linalool present in lavender help to relax the airways, reducing congestion and inflammation that can exacerbate a dry cough. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can help to ease tension and anxiety related to coughing, making it easier to rest and recover. Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic coughing, and alleviate congestion and discomfort in the chest and throat.

Antioxidant and antibacterial properties: Lavender's antioxidants and antibacterial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses that may be contributing to the dry cough, while also protecting the respiratory tract from further damage.

Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or four teaspoons of fresh lavender leaves and stems.
Combine the harvested lavender with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender solids and discard.

Combine one teaspoon of the herb material with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Enjoy the warm tea after it has cooled slightly while taking care to avoid any remaining sediment.

Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing relief from the scratchiness associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for one cup of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of the herb with one cup of boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or mug, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired, as it is known to soothe a dry cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways. The tea's anethole compound has been shown to relax the smooth muscles in the bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and easing congestion. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory effects help soothe the irritated mucous membranes, providing fast relief from dry coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic properties help to relax the airways and reduce spasms in the throat, providing relief from dry, hacking coughs.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can contribute to dry coughs and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing and relieving dry cough symptoms.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural mucolytic properties that help break down mucus and ease its removal from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from your garden or local health food store weighing approximately 20 grams.
Rinse the harvested herbs gently with cold running water removing any dirt or debris then pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the rinsed herbs with boiling water in a large saucepan pouring 250 milliliters of water for every 10 grams of herbs used.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 5 minutes and up to 10 minutes depending on your desired strength of tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup discarding the solids and serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with dry cough because it provides a soothing warmth that relaxes the throat and reduces irritation. The natural anti-inflammatory properties in herbs such as ginger, peppermint, and thyme help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it easier to expectorate mucus. Additionally, herbal tea's humidifying effects help to loosen phlegm and bring relief from dry coughing fits, promoting a good night's sleep and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Soothing mucilages: Herbs like marshmallow root, slippery elm, and licorice root contain soothing mucilages that coat and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving dryness and discomfort associated with a dry cough.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbs like ginger, turmeric, and cinnamon contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to a dry, hacking cough.
Expectorant properties: Herbs like thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing a dry cough and reducing inflammation in the throat.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help to relax the bronchial muscles, relieving coughing fits and promoting mucus clearance.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves or one green tea bag.
Heat filtered water in a teapot to a boil then let it cool for two minutes.
Steep the green tea leaves in the hot water for three to five minutes.
Add one half teaspoon of dried honey and stir well until fully dissolved.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, have a soothing effect on the throat and bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughing. Additionally, maypop tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the production of excess mucus, providing long-lasting relief from dry, hacking coughs.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: Maypop tea contains alkaloids such as pasleythrine, which have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in maypop tea, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
Saponins: Saponins in maypop tea have antitussive properties, meaning they can help suppress or relieve coughing by decreasing the sensitivity of the respiratory tract and calming the cough reflex.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated throats, making it an effective treatment for dry coughs.
Rhyzomes: Containing compounds like glycosides and flavonoids, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce throat congestion and relieve dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers in the early morning or evening when they are most fragrant.
Dry the harvested material by tying it in small bunches and hanging upside down in a warm well-ventilated area for 1 week.
Grind dried passiflora incarnata into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to create a consistent texture.
Steep 2 teaspoons of the ground passiflora incarnata in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving hot or warm.
Consume the tea 3 times daily as needed, allowing at least 2 hours between servings to maximize benefits for dry cough relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop teas helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling in the throat and bronchial tubes, making it easier to expel mucus. Additionally, hyssop contains compounds that have antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles in the airways and calm dry, hacking coughs. By soothing and clearing the respiratory tract, hyssop teas provide natural relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing. This allows for easier clearing of the airways and reduces discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to dryness and irritation that exacerbate coughs. By reducing inflammation, hyssop tea helps soothe the throat and lungs.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Hyssop's antiviral and antibacterial components help combat underlying infections that may be causing or contributing to a dry cough, such as the common cold or bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making them effective in treating dry coughs.
Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop plant contain expectorant properties, which help loosen mucus and reduce congestion associated with dry coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers in a small bowl.
Measure out one teaspoon of the herb per cup of boiling water using digital scale.
Steep the herb mixture for seven to ten minutes in boiling water with timer set.
Strain the liquid through fine-mesh sieve into large heat-resistant measuring cup.
Allow the tea to cool and then store it in refrigerator for up to three days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry cough that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and allergies. The soothing properties of herbs like slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root help to calm irritated airways and reduce inflammation. Teas made from these herbs can also ease sinus pressure and congestion, providing relief from symptoms similar to a dry cough.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry cough are natural remedies that help alleviate dry cough symptoms by soothing the throat and reducing inflammation. The best herbal teas for dry cough include Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Verbascum thapsus, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Sambucus nigra, and Rosmarinus officinalis. If left untreated, dry cough can disrupt sleep, reduce productivity, and lead to fatigue, anxiety, and respiratory complications, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with dry cough is a blend of Slippery Elm, Licorice Root, and Thyme. Slippery Elm soothes the mucous membranes, Licorice Root provides anti-inflammatory properties to reduce irritation, and Thyme has antimicrobial properties to combat infection. These teas work together to provide relief from dry cough, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Drinking a warm infusion of this blend can help alleviate discomfort and promote a speedy recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Alternative therapies in health and medicine, herbal teas from traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) were found to be beneficial in treating upper airway cough syndrome (UACS), which is a common cause of chronic cough. The study, which included 1355 participants and 16 studies, found that patients who received CMH treatments showed a higher TCM recovery rate, better relief of primary symptoms, including cough and postnasal dripping, and a reduction in physical signs compared to those who received Western medicine. Specifically, the CMH treatments resulted in a 3-9 point reduction in cough symptom scores, with higher scores indicating more frequent and severe cough, indicating that herbal teas from TCM may be a safe and effective alternative for treating dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with dry cough because it contains high levels of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound. When inhaled, eucalyptol helps to break down and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughs. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus teas have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections that can cause coughs. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm dry, irritated throats, providing quick relief from the discomfort of a dry cough.

Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The liquorice root, a key component of this tea, contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that calm the throat and reduce inflammation. This helps to break down mucus and phlegm, providing relief from a dry, irritated cough. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, promoting a smoother and more comfortable breathing experience.

Echinacea purpurea teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The tea is rich in compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which help to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects can help to boost the body's defenses against infections that cause dry cough, such as the common cold and flu. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms.

This results in a reduction in coughing and an improvement in respiratory function, allowing for easier breathing and a more rapid recovery from dry coughs. Its natural properties make it a popular remedy for dry cough relief.

Verbascum thapsus teas helps with dry cough because it contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that have soothing and emollient properties. When inhaled, these mucilages help to calm and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to a dry cough. The tea's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing and promoting a more comfortable respiratory experience. Mentha x piperita teas helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The peppermint oil present in the tea has a soothing effect on the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough. Additionally, mentha x piperita's natural menthol content helps to open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the tightness and irritation caused by dry cough, providing fast and effective relief.

Zingiber officinale teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compound, gingerol, in the tea helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, providing relief from dry coughs caused by colds, flu, or allergies. This natural remedy promotes a soothing and comforting effect, making it an effective way to alleviate dry cough symptoms.

Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing properties and natural anti-inflammatory compounds. Sambucus nigra teas helps with dry cough because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea is made from the elderberry plant, which contains flavonoids and anthocyanins that have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat viral infections. These compounds help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity of dry coughs. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms. Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with dry cough because it contains compounds such as carnosic acid and rosmanol, which have potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
